com.hissain.jscipy.signal

Class Waveform

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hissain.jscipy.signal.Waveform

  • public class Waveform
extends java.lang.Object
    Waveform generation utilities, mirroring scipy.signal waveform functions.

    Provides methods to generate common test and analysis signals: chirp, square wave, sawtooth wave, Gaussian pulse, and unit impulse.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Waveform() 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      double[] chirp(double[] t, double f0, double t1, double f1)
      Frequency-swept cosine generator using linear sweep (default).
      double[] chirp(double[] t, double f0, double t1, double f1, java.lang.String method)
      Frequency-swept cosine generator (chirp signal).
      double[] gausspulse(double[] t, double fc)
      Return a Gaussian modulated sinusoid with default bandwidth of 0.5.
      double[] gausspulse(double[] t, double fc, double bw)
      Return a Gaussian modulated sinusoid (Gaussian pulse).
      double[] sawtooth(double[] t)
      Return a periodic sawtooth wave (width=1).
      double[] sawtooth(double[] t, double width)
      Return a periodic sawtooth or triangle wave.
      double[] square(double[] t)
      Return a periodic square wave with duty cycle 0.5.
      double[] square(double[] t, double duty)
      Return a periodic square wave with the given duty cycle.
      double[] unitImpulse(int shape)
      Return a unit impulse at index 0 (default).
      double[] unitImpulse(int shape, int idx)
      Return a unit impulse (discrete delta function) signal.

        public Waveform()

    • Method Detail

      • chirp

        public double[] chirp(double[] t,
                      double f0,
                      double t1,
                      double f1,
                      java.lang.String method)
        Frequency-swept cosine generator (chirp signal). Matches scipy.signal.chirp.
        Parameters:
        t - Time array (seconds).
        f0 - Frequency at time t=0 (Hz).
        t1 - Time at which f1 is defined (seconds).
        f1 - Frequency at time t1 (Hz).
        method - Sweep type: "linear", "quadratic", "logarithmic", or "hyperbolic".
        Returns:
        Chirp signal evaluated at each time in t.

      • chirp

        public double[] chirp(double[] t,
                      double f0,
                      double t1,
                      double f1)
        Frequency-swept cosine generator using linear sweep (default).
        Parameters:
        t - Time array (seconds).
        f0 - Frequency at time t=0 (Hz).
        t1 - Time at which f1 is defined (seconds).
        f1 - Frequency at time t1 (Hz).
        Returns:
        Chirp signal evaluated at each time in t.

      • square

        public double[] square(double[] t,
                       double duty)
        Return a periodic square wave with the given duty cycle. Matches scipy.signal.square.

        The function takes values +1 and -1. The duty cycle is the fraction of the period during which the signal is +1.

        Parameters:
        t - Time array. The period is 2*pi radians.
        duty - Duty cycle (0 to 1). Default 0.5.
        Returns:
        Square wave evaluated at each element of t.

      • square

        public double[] square(double[] t)
        Return a periodic square wave with duty cycle 0.5.
        Parameters:
        t - Time array. The period is 2*pi radians.
        Returns:
        Square wave evaluated at each element of t.

      • sawtooth

        public double[] sawtooth(double[] t,
                         double width)
        Return a periodic sawtooth or triangle wave. Matches scipy.signal.sawtooth.

        The sawtooth waveform has a period of 2*pi, rising from -1 to +1 in [0, 2*pi*width] and then dropping from +1 to -1 in [2*pi*width, 2*pi].

        Parameters:
        t - Time array. The period is 2*pi radians.
        width - Width of the rising ramp (0 to 1). width=1 gives a pure sawtooth, width=0.5 gives a triangle wave.
        Returns:
        Sawtooth wave evaluated at each element of t.

      • sawtooth

        public double[] sawtooth(double[] t)
        Return a periodic sawtooth wave (width=1).
        Parameters:
        t - Time array. The period is 2*pi radians.
        Returns:
        Sawtooth wave evaluated at each element of t.

      • gausspulse

        public double[] gausspulse(double[] t,
                           double fc,
                           double bw)
        Return a Gaussian modulated sinusoid (Gaussian pulse). Matches scipy.signal.gausspulse with retquad=False, retenv=False.

        The pulse has a center frequency fc and a fractional bandwidth bw at the -6 dB level.

        Parameters:
        t - Time array (seconds).
        fc - Center frequency (Hz). Default 1000.
        bw - Fractional bandwidth (ratio). Default 0.5.
        Returns:
        Gaussian modulated sinusoid evaluated at each element of t.

      • gausspulse

        public double[] gausspulse(double[] t,
                           double fc)
        Return a Gaussian modulated sinusoid with default bandwidth of 0.5.
        Parameters:
        t - Time array (seconds).
        fc - Center frequency (Hz).
        Returns:
        Gaussian modulated sinusoid evaluated at each element of t.

      • unitImpulse

        public double[] unitImpulse(int shape,
                            int idx)
        Return a unit impulse (discrete delta function) signal. Matches scipy.signal.unit_impulse.

        Returns a signal of length shape that is zero everywhere except at index idx where it equals 1.

        Parameters:
        shape - Total length of the output signal.
        idx - Index at which the impulse occurs. Use -1 for center (shape/2).
        Returns:
        Unit impulse signal of length shape.

      • unitImpulse

        public double[] unitImpulse(int shape)
        Return a unit impulse at index 0 (default).
        Parameters:
        shape - Total length of the output signal.
        Returns:
        Unit impulse signal with impulse at index 0.
